title: Odontochilus perpusillus
taxonomy: Odontochilus perpusillus is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family comprises approximately 30,000 known species of orchids. The genus Odontochilus is a small group of orchids, mainly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The species Odontochilus perpusillus is considered a rare and endangered orchid due to its limited geographical distribution and habitat loss. Further classification of Odontochilus perpusillus may be more specific and could include subgenus or subspecies designations.

intro: Odontochilus perpusillus, a species of orchid, is a rare and endangered plant native to the tropical forests of Asia. Traditionally, Odontochilus perpusillus has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its purported health benefits, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.
